title: Eight-Point Arc
author: Nigel Watts
source: Writing a Novel and Getting Published, 1996.

lineage.note: A clean, novel-oriented restatement of the same monomyth spine. Distinctive contribution: 'Critical Choice' as an active decision, not a passive sweeping-along.

stages.name: Stasis
stages.function: The everyday world before the story starts.

stages.name: Trigger
stages.function: An event sets the protagonist's quest in motion.

stages.name: The Quest
stages.function: The protagonist sets out to resolve the disturbance.

stages.name: Surprise
stages.function: Obstacles, challenges, and revelations along the way.

stages.name: Critical Choice
stages.function: The protagonist must actively choose — under pressure — between competing goods or evils. (Distinctive Watts emphasis.)

stages.name: Climax
stages.function: Highest stakes confrontation; consequences of the critical choice play out.

stages.name: Reversal
stages.function: The change brought about by the climax — a new state.

applicability.limitations: Hinges entirely on a single 'Critical Choice' — stories without a clear single decision moment fit poorly.
